role_title: Senior Associate, New Verticals - Grocery Strategy & Operations

location.office_time_required: a few days a month in person

level_signals: Base pay range $87K-$128K consistent with mid-level IC at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 2-4+ years of experience. You've succeeded in similar cultures that are highly analytical and cross-functional (whether in tech, operations, strategy, consulting, banking, or a related experience).
you're comfortable pulling your own data in SQL and building models in excel.
Build models using Excel and SQL to evaluate the economic, operational and strategic costs and benefits of various programs and experiments
You solve problems from first principles. You have a talent for synthesizing complex problems, can translate high-level goals into actionable plans, and enjoy implementing a process

You're energized by AI tools — and you actively experiment with them. You don't wait to be told how to use a new tool; you've already tried it. You bring a builder's mindset to AI: using it to automate the repetitive, accelerate the analytical, and unlock work that would otherwise require a larger team.
You'll partner with and influence cross-functional teams including growth, quality, design, marketing, partnerships and product team to build and scale the Grocery business.
You're always looking for opportunities to better your work product and own the outcome – both the good and the bad
You're unafraid of ambiguity. Rapid change and learning is exciting to you, and you thrive in ambiguous, startup environment
You have a completed Bachelor's Degree.

scope.surface: DoorDash Grocery business within the New Verticals team (Grocery, Convenience, Alcohol, Retail delivery marketplace)
primary_metrics_inferred: Consumer acquisition, retention, and engagement in Grocery
Economic and operational costs/benefits of programs and experiments
core_responsibilities: Launch and manage growth and quality initiatives
Dive into data to identify opportunities informing product strategy
Own tracking dashboards and manage week-over-week metrics
Build models using Excel and SQL to evaluate programs and experiments
Transform strategies into operational experiments with measurable outcomes
Use AI tools to rapidly prototype consumer-facing ideas, create mocks for user research, synthesize qualitative feedback, and develop product visions
Partner with and influence cross-functional teams

green_flags: Clear product surface and team mission (Grocery within New Verticals)
Explicit AI tooling expectations set upfront — signals a forward-thinking team culture
Hybrid policy is light (only a few days a month in person)
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Strong emphasis on ownership and entrepreneurial scope
red_flags: Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Role requires physical presence in NY, SF, or Seattle — no fully remote option
The JD conflates '2-4+ years' as both the lower and upper bound — the '+' after 4 is ambiguous about true ceiling expectations
No explicit mention of grocery or retail industry experience as preferred — domain knowledge expectations are unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior Associate', years range, and comp)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from statement 'Base salary is localized according to an employee's work location')
ambiguities: '2-4+ years' phrasing is internally ambiguous — the '+' suggests no hard upper ceiling, but the lower bound is 2 years; treated as minimum 2, maximum 4 with soft upper bound
No nice-to-haves were explicitly flagged under 'preferred', 'bonus', or 'nice to have' language; all qualifications in the JD are framed as requirements
